title: Crispy Light Greek Shrimp Fritters
description: Sweet, pillowy, light Greek dish featuring shrimp stock, perfect for a quick snack or dessert.
instructions: Combine shrimp stock, Greek yogurt, feta cheese, lemon zest, and chopped fresh dill in a bowl. Mix well until a smooth batter forms. Add breadcrumbs, egg whites, salt, basil (if using), and paprika. Mix until just combined. Do not overmix. The batter should still be slightly lumpy.
Heat about 2-3 inches of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the shrimp fritter batter into the hot oil, being careful not to overcrowd the pan.
Fry the shrimp fritters for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they turn golden brown and crispy. Adjust the heat as needed to prevent burning.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried shrimp fritters from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Serve the crispy shrimp fritters warm with a side of your favorite dipping sauce, such as a tzatziki sauce or a simple lemon sauce.
Optional: Garnish with additional chopped fresh dill and a sprinkle of paprika.
